data.title: South Korea's Central Bank to Hire Experts for Digital Asset Analysis
data.description:
South Korea's central bank has announced plans to hire 10 new employees, with some positions requiring at least five years of experience in the cryptocurrency industry. According to ChainCatcher, these new hires will begin as early as July this year, taking on roles as digital asset market analysts to study the impact of cryptocurrencies, stablecoins, and tokenized assets on financial stability.

This recruitment drive coincides with the central bank's revival of its CBDC initiative, known as the Han River project. The project was previously halted last year due to user feedback on its inconvenience. The new phase of the project will focus on government and corporate applications, with the aim of distributing 25% of government subsidies via digital won by 2030. A pilot payment system is planned to launch in the first half of 2026.

The head of the Bank of Korea's digital currency planning team stated that stablecoins and CBDCs are complementary rather than competitive. This initiative aligns with upcoming legislation that will lift the long-standing ban on the issuance of cryptocurrencies and stablecoins within South Korea.

data.title: Whale Closes ETH Long Positions with Modest Profit
data.description:
On March 26, a significant Ethereum (ETH) holder closed all long positions, according to BlockBeats. The positions were closed at an average price of $2,159, resulting in a modest profit of approximately $15,000, which likely covered the order fees. The total profit for the entire cycle reached $120,000.

The long positions were initially opened on March 20 at an average price of $2,140, with an initial position size of around $8.67 million. The holder continued to roll over and add to the positions, increasing the average price to above $2,150, with the peak position size reaching $8.67 million.

data.title: Solana's Core Team Introduces Constellation Protocol to Enhance Blockchain Speed
data.description:
Solana's core development team, Anza, has launched the Constellation protocol, significantly reducing Solana's block cycle to 50 milliseconds. According to PANews, this advancement positions Solana as the fastest blockchain in terms of protocol-level economic rhythm.

The Constellation protocol replaces the single leader model with a multi-concurrent proposer system. This change eliminates exclusive control over transaction inclusion and ordering, enforcing fairness at the protocol level without relying on trusted third parties or client modifications. The economic model for validators remains largely unchanged.

data.title: Binance to Delist UTK from Margin Trading
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the platform will delist UTK as a borrowable asset from both Cross Margin and Isolated Margin pairs on 2026-03-30 at 06:00 (UTC). This decision affects the UTK/USDT trading pairs in both margin categories. Effective immediately, users will no longer be able to transfer UTK via manual transfers or Auto-Transfer Mode into their Margin Accounts. Users with outstanding liabilities in UTK may only transfer up to the amount of their liabilities, minus any available collateral.

On 2026-03-27 at 06:00 (UTC), Binance Margin will suspend borrowings for the UTK cross margin and isolated margin pairs. By 2026-03-30 at 06:00 (UTC), Binance will close all user positions, conduct automatic settlements, and cancel pending orders for the isolated margin pairs, which will then be removed. If users hold both collateral and liabilities in UTK on cross margin, the collateral will be used to repay the liabilities. Depending on the Collateral Margin Level (CML), remaining tokens may be transferred to Spot Accounts or sold.

For users holding UTK as liabilities, if the CML is below 2, pending orders will be canceled, and other collateral tokens will be sold to repay the liabilities. Users are advised to close positions and transfer assets to Spot Accounts before the delisting to avoid potential losses. Portfolio Margin users should transfer UTK out of Margin Accounts and monitor the Unified Maintenance Margin Ratio (uniMMR) to prevent liquidation. Any remaining UTK in Portfolio Margin Accounts after 2026-03-30 at 06:00 (UTC) will be liquidated and converted to USDT or other stablecoins. Binance emphasizes that it will not be responsible for any losses incurred during this process.

data.title: Whale Withdraws Significant Ethereum Amount from Centralized Exchange
data.description:
On March 26, a major cryptocurrency whale withdrew 11,999 ETH from a centralized exchange, marking a significant move after a month of inactivity. According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, the whale has staked the withdrawn Ethereum.

Currently, the whale holds a total of 22,618 ETH, with a floating profit of $1.2 million. This activity highlights the ongoing strategies employed by large holders in the cryptocurrency market.

data.title: CFTC Chairman Highlights Importance of Crypto Futures in U.S. Policy
data.description: The Chairman of the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC), Michael Selig, emphasized the significance of reintroducing 'real' cryptocurrency perpetual futures to the United States as a vital element of the agency's innovation policy agenda. According to PANews, this move is seen as a crucial step in aligning the U.S. with global advancements in the cryptocurrency sector. Selig's remarks underscore the CFTC's commitment to fostering innovation while ensuring regulatory compliance in the rapidly evolving digital asset landscape.

data.title: South Korea to Stabilize Bond Market with Government Bond Buyback
data.description: South Korea has announced measures to stabilize its bond market, according to Jin10. The country's Ministry of Finance stated that it will repurchase government bonds worth 5 trillion Korean won. Following the announcement, South Korean government bond futures experienced a slight increase.

data.title: TD Securities: Oil Price Fluctuations Unlikely to Prompt Aggressive Fed Action
data.description: TD Securities analysts have indicated that the recent volatility in oil prices is unlikely to lead the Federal Reserve to adopt aggressive policy measures. According to Jin10, while the market has begun to factor in the risk of interest rate hikes due to high inflation expectations, TD Securities suggests that the Fed is more likely to adopt a 'wait-and-see' approach. The leadership at the Federal Reserve remains inclined towards implementing rate cuts in late 2026. The bank further noted that as long as long-term inflation expectations remain stable and second-round effects are controlled, the Fed will overlook the impact of this energy shock.

data.title: Citigroup: Investors are overly optimistic that the US-Iran issue will be resolved soon.
data.description: Citigroup's chief investment officer, Kate Moore, believes investors seem overly optimistic that the US-Iran issue is about to be resolved. She stated on Wednesday, "Some of the price volatility we've experienced, especially in the last two days, I think actually indicates a great deal of optimism that the issue will be resolved, and that this energy shock won't trigger widespread inflation. Frankly, that worries me a little." She added, "I want to be extremely cautious about how we construct our portfolios. Because what we want to build right now is a resilient portfolio, and we must also ensure that we can withstand inflationary risks, as well as the risk of a conflict that may be more persistent than current market optimists anticipate." (Jinshi)

data.title: India Resumes LPG Imports from Iran Amid Middle East Conflict
data.description: Indian Oil has purchased liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) from Iran for the first time in nearly eight years, sources revealed. Bloomberg posted on X that this move comes as India seeks to prevent a deepening shortage of the essential cooking fuel, exacerbated by ongoing conflict in the Middle East. The decision to resume imports from Iran highlights India's efforts to secure energy supplies amidst regional instability. The Middle East conflict has disrupted supply chains, prompting India to explore alternative sources to meet domestic demand. This development marks a significant shift in India's energy procurement strategy, reflecting the urgency to address potential shortages.

data.title: Morgan Stanley's Bitcoin ETF Receives NYSE Listing Announcement
data.description: On March 26, Bloomberg's senior ETF analyst Eric Balchunas reported that Morgan Stanley's Bitcoin ETF, identified by the ticker MSBT, has received an official listing announcement from the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE). According to BlockBeats, such announcements typically indicate that the product is nearing its official launch.

data.title: South Korean Officials and Families Continue to Hold Virtual Assets in 2026 Disclosure
data.description: The 2026 regular asset disclosure has revealed that officials in the Lee Jae-myung administration, along with their families, continue to hold virtual assets. According to NS3.AI, Lee Min-joo reported holding cryptocurrency valued at 170.32 million won. Additionally, President Lee Jae-myung's eldest son disclosed holdings worth 41.06 million won, primarily in XRP and Tether.

data.title: Cryptex Digital Market Cap ETF Seeks Approval for Crypto Holdings
data.description: Eric Balchunas, senior ETF Analyst at Bloomberg Intelligence, posted on X about a new filing for the Cryptex Digital Market Cap ETF. The ETF, which has chosen the ticker $BAGZ, is structured using a Cayman subsidiary and aims to hold approximately 30 cryptocurrencies directly. This approach could potentially surpass the current offerings in the market, providing a broader range of crypto assets for investors.

data.title: Japan to Join Military Exercises in the Philippines for the First Time Since WWII
data.description: Japanese ground forces will participate in military exercises in the Philippines next month, marking a significant development in post-World War II history. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this as a deepening of ties between Manila and Tokyo. This collaboration underscores the strengthening of defense relations between the two nations.

data.title: DoubleZero Token Unlock Scheduled for April
data.description: DoubleZero (2Z) is set to unlock approximately 139.7 million tokens on April 2 at 13:00 UTC+8. According to ChainCatcher, this token release is part of the ongoing schedule for DoubleZero's asset management.

data.title: A whale withdrew $15.68 million from OKX PAXG
data.description: According to Lookonchain's monitoring, a whale withdrew 3,477 PAXG from OKX, worth $15.68 million.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Seeks Swift Resolution to Iran Conflict
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has expressed a desire to avoid a prolonged conflict with Iran, aiming to conclude the situation within the coming weeks. According to NS3.AI, Trump has yet to establish a definitive strategy for ending the war, as peace negotiations are still in their initial phases. White House Press Secretary Levitt stated that Trump remains dedicated to the military goal of striking Iran, with victory as his ultimate aim.

data.title: Base Metals Decline Amid Uncertainty in U.S.-Iran Negotiations
data.description: Copper prices, along with other base metals, experienced a decline as investors closely watched the ongoing uncertainty surrounding negotiations between the United States and Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the market's sensitivity to geopolitical developments that could potentially impact global trade dynamics. The talks aim to address and possibly resolve tensions, which have been a source of concern for investors. The outcome of these negotiations is seen as pivotal in determining future market trends for base metals. As the situation unfolds, market participants remain cautious, awaiting further clarity on the geopolitical front.

data.title: China Advocates Political Solutions Amid Global Economic Concerns
data.description: China's third-ranking official, Zhao Leji, has emphasized the importance of resolving international disputes through political means. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Beijing's role as a stabilizing force in the face of ongoing tensions in Iran that pose risks to the global economy. Zhao's remarks underscore China's diplomatic approach to international conflicts, aiming to mitigate economic disruptions worldwide.

data.title: Fenix Warns of Diesel Supply Constraints Impacting Australian Mining Operations
data.description: Fenix Resources has issued a warning about the constraints on diesel supply due to the ongoing conflict in Iran, which are beginning to affect operations across Australia's mining sector. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the iron ore miner is being forced to scale back certain activities as a result of these supply issues. The company is closely monitoring the situation and assessing the potential impact on its operations. The diesel supply constraints are a growing concern for the mining industry, which relies heavily on fuel for its operations. Fenix is taking steps to mitigate the impact, but the situation remains challenging as the conflict continues to disrupt supply chains.
